rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FARNSWORTH AND MICHELLE FARNSWORTH TRUST VS. SIMO M. AMZIL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff And Cross Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Decree Of Partition By Sale And For Appointment Of Partition Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF RICHARD FARNSWORTH AND MICHELLE FARNSWORTH TRUST Plaintiff And Cross Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Decree Of Partition By Sale And For Appointment Of Partition Referee is GRANTED. There is no dispute as to the partition in this action, a remedy sought by both the Complaint and the Cross-Complaint. Assuming that parties entered into an agreement (the Court makes no such finding), the partition by sale is within the purported agreed-up timeframe of "within/no later than three years from November 2022. Since the parties cannot agree on a referee, each side to submit names of three referees (and CVs/hourly rate) no later than November 16, 2023. Plaintiff shall also provide a proposed order on this motion leaving the partition referee name blank no later than November 22, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 3050-3058 CESAR CHAVEZ, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. ANTHONY MARTIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT ANTHONY MARTIN, TIMOTHY MARTIN DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:30 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Moving party failed to show that any of the required information has not been provided to the tenants or that any of the information that was required to be provided was incorrect/incomplete, etc. Moving party also failed to show that superfluous and gratuitous language in a "Cover Letter" renders the termination to tenancy under the Ellis Act ineffective. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and breach of contract and is not uncertain. The Court notes that this action has a "CUD" designation but includes a non-summary proceeding cause of action outside of CCP 1159 et seq provisions. For all procedural purposes this action shall proceed on a general civil track and will be referred to Department 610 for case management.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BEHROUZ HALALI VS. RODRIGO MENDOZA LUNA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT RODRIGO LUNA DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:30 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges as one of the "key terms of the parties' oral rental agreement" a provision that "Rodrigo Mendoza Luna has exclusive use of one bedroom in the Premises," this makes Mr. Luna a tenant under both the state and the local law, and not a "lodger" as the required element under CC 1946.5 is missing "right of access to all areas of the dwelling unit occupied by the lodger." "The exclusive use of one bedroom" allegation is incompatible with any "right of access" to that bedroom. Plaintiff additional fails to provide any legal authority by which a tenancy agreement is transformed into a lodging agreement by an unilateral act of one of the contracting parties. Furthermore, even if allegations regarding defendant's "lodger" status were sufficient, Plaintiff would still have to plead and prove compliance with SFRO 37.9(c), which has not been done and there is no suggestion in the opposition that it could be done. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOANNA LUO VS. XUE YING DU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT XUE DU, DEREK KONG, RUIBO ZHENG, GOUCHAO DU DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:30 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Plaintiff alleges a landlord-tenant relationship that is being terminated under the provision of the Rent Ordinance. Any issues regarding existence of the landlord-tenant relationship (e.g. whether the plaintiffs are "landlord" making the defendants "tenants" of a residential unit, which may or may not be legal) and applicability of SFRO (in light of possible lack of a landlord-tenant relationship) are issues of fact that will not be addressed on a demurrer. Furthermore, the dominant motive and good faith are sufficiently alleged and cannot be disputed on a demurrer. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 816 GEARY I5, LP VS. JEROME JOSEPH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 8, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT JEROME JOSEPH DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Hearing at 10:30 a.m. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. On the demurrer the Court does not look beyond the allegations in the Complaint (attachments thereto). The Court will not be considering the declaration submitted in opposition. As Complaint is currently alleged the Plaintiff is pursuing this action on a 3 Day Notice to Quit for Nuisance without an opportunity to cure and asserting the exception from compliance with SFRO 37.9(o) (paragraph 9 of the operative Notice). Plaintiff's case and defendant's demurrer at this time are framed by this complaint. This complaint currently states sufficient facts to assert a cause of action for unlawful detainer. If this is not what Plaintiff intended to allege, Plaintiff shall properly seek leave to amend to allege service of the 10 Day Warning Notice. Only at that time the issue will be properly on a demurrer was defendant's argument that such warning notice was not sufficient. Furthermore, the allegation in the complaint regarding SFRO 37.9(n) does not render the complaint uncertain as Defendant may admit or deny this allegation in their answer. The Court might have considered striking this allegation had Defendant filed a motion to strike. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
